title: Make first contact with the Youth Entrepreneurship Scheme
description: Tell the Youth Entrepreneurship Scheme (YES) about your business idea so they can offer support.
summary: The Youth Entrepreneurship Scheme (YES) helps young Barbadians turn ideas into businesses. It is run by the Division of Youth and Culture in the Office of the Prime Minister.

The First Contact form is the starting point. You tell YES about yourself, your business idea (or your existing business), and what kind of support you are looking for – training, funding, mentoring, or just someone to talk through your plans with.
Once your form is in, a YES officer will get in touch within two weeks to arrange a meeting. The meeting is usually one-to-one and runs for about an hour.
The First Contact form is not an application for funding. It is the start of a conversation. Funding, training and mentoring all come later, once YES understands what you need.
who_can_apply: people with a business idea, or an existing business that is under 3 years old

items: your full name, address and date of birth
a short summary of your business idea or current business
what stage your idea is at
the kind of support you are looking for (training, funding, mentoring)

how_long: You will be contacted within 2 weeks to arrange your first meeting.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Youth Entrepreneurship Scheme First Contact Form to the Division of Youth and Culture, Prime Minister’s Office.

contact.agency: Division of Youth and Culture, Office of the Prime Minister
contact.address:
Sky Mall, Haggatt Hall
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Aged 18 to 35’ eligibility is the commonly-quoted YES range.
‘An existing business that is under 3 years old’ is commonly used. The form may use different wording.
‘You will be contacted within 2 weeks’ is an estimate.
Contact details for the Division of Youth and Culture are illustrative.
